basic_details.job_title: NBEMS Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for Group A, B & C Posts | Deputy Director, Junior Programmer, Accountant, Stenographer & Junior Assistant
basic_details.organization_name: National Board of Examinations in Medical Sciences (NBEMS)
basic_details.post_name: Deputy Director (Medical), Junior Programmer, Junior Accountant, Stenographer, Junior Assistant
basic_details.short_information: Before diving into the detailed eligibility and application process, here is a snapshot of all the essential information from the official notification at a glance. This helps candidates quickly assess whether they qualify and what the key deadlines are.

age_limit.details: Understanding age limits clearly helps candidates confirm their eligibility before investing time in application preparation. Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD and other categories will be as per Government of India norms. | Note: Age relaxation for reserved category candidates (SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen) will be as per Government of India directives. Candidates are advised to verify the detailed relaxation provisions from the full official notification on www.natboard.edu.in.

salary_details.details: Central Government pay scales at NBEMS are governed by the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC) Pay Matrix. Below is the pay level assigned to each post, which determines the basic pay and overall salary package including DA, HRA, and other allowances. | Note: Actual salary in hand will be higher due to applicable Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), Transport Allowance, and other Central Government perks and benefits as per rules.

selection_process: Knowing the selection process in advance helps candidates prepare their strategy early. NBEMS will conduct a written examination for most posts
along with a skill test for the Stenographer post.
the selection process for NBEMS Recruitment 2026 is expected to include the following stages:
Written Examination - For all posts as prescribed by NBEMS (the tentative exam date is 5th & 6th September 2026). Skill/Proficiency Test - For the Stenographer post
candidates will be required to qualify a test in General English
Shorthand & Typewriting conducted by the Board. Document Verification - Shortlisted candidates will be required to produce original documents to verify eligibility.

and selection criteria for each post will be published on the official NBEMS website www.natboard.edu.in. Candidates are strongly advised to refer to the full detailed vacancy notice for complete selection process information.
how_to_apply: The application process for NBEMS Recruitment 2026 is entirely online. No offline or postal applications will be accepted. The step-by-step process to apply is outlined below based on the official notice.
Visit the Official Website - Go to www.natboard.edu.in to access the recruitment section.
Wait for Registration Activation - Online registration will be activated w.e.f. 29th June 2026.

Read the Detailed Notice - Before registering, carefully read the full detailed vacancy notice available on the NBEMS website for complete eligibility conditions, reservation details, syllabus, and terms.
Register Online - Click on the online application link for the relevant post and complete the registration process.
Fill in the Application Form - Enter all required personal, educational, and professional details accurately.
Upload Documents - Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature, and supporting documents as required.
Pay Application Fee - Pay the applicable examination/application fee online (fee details will be in the full notification on the website).
Submit & Save Confirmation - Submit the form and save/print the confirmation page for future reference.
Apply Online at: www.natboard.edu.in (Registration active from 29 June 2026)
important_instructions: Candidates must ensure they are fully aware of all terms and conditions before applying. The following points are directly based on the official NBEMS vacancy notice and are important for a smooth application experience.

The number of vacancies is liable to change - the final count will be confirmed in the detailed notice. Reservation in all posts will be strictly as per Government of India norms. The full detailed vacancy notice - including last date, syllabus, reservation details, and other conditions - is published on www.natboard.edu.in. The tentative date of examination is 5th and 6th September 2026. Online registration will be activated w.e.f. 29th June 2026 - no applications will be accepted before that date. Candidates must qualify the examination prescribed by NBEMS for Junior Accountant, Junior Assistant, and Stenographer posts.

faq.question: Q1. What is NBEMS Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: NBEMS Recruitment 2026 refers to the official Vacancy Notice (No. A-12011/3/2025-ESTB, dated 02 June 2026) released by the National Board of Examinations in Medical Sciences for regular appointment to 53 posts across Group A, B, and C categories, including Deputy Director (Medical), Junior Programmer, Junior Accountant, Stenographer, and Junior Assistant.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in NBEMS Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are a total of 53 vacancies in NBEMS Recruitment 2026 - 2 for Deputy Director (Medical), 1 for Junior Programmer, 4 for Junior Accountant, 7 for Stenographer, and 39 for Junior Assistant. The vacancy count is liable to change as per the official notification.

faq.question: Q3. When will NBEMS online registration open in 2026?
faq.answer: The online registration for NBEMS Recruitment 2026 will be activated with effect from 29th June 2026 on the official NBEMS website at www.natboard.edu.in.

faq.question: Q4. What is the exam date for NBEMS Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The tentative date of examination for NBEMS Recruitment 2026 is scheduled to be held on 5th and 6th September 2026, as mentioned in the official vacancy notice.

faq.question: Q5. What is the eligibility for NBEMS Deputy Director (Medical) post?
faq.answer: For the Deputy Director (Medical) post, the candidate must be below 35 years of age and must hold a recognized Postgraduate Medical qualification approved under the Indian Medical Council Act-1956. Desirable qualifications include training in medical education technology and knowledge of modern evaluation techniques such as MCQ generation and MEQ analysis.

faq.question: Q6. What is the qualification required for NBEMS Junior Assistant post?
faq.answer: For the Junior Assistant post, candidates must have passed the Senior Secondary Examination (12th of 10+2) from a Board/University recognized by Central/State Govt./UT Administration, must have proficiency in computers and basic software (Windows, LAN architecture), and must qualify the examination as may be prescribed by NBEMS.

faq.question: Q7. What is the pay scale for NBEMS Junior Assistant 2026?
faq.answer: The Junior Assistant post is placed at Pay Matrix Level 2 under the 7th CPC, with a basic pay range of approximately ₹19,900 to ₹63,200. In addition to basic pay, employees receive Dearness Allowance, HRA, Transport Allowance, and other Central Government benefits.

faq.question: Q8. What is the age limit for NBEMS Stenographer post?
faq.answer: The age limit for the NBEMS Stenographer post is 18 to 27 years. Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD and other reserved categories is applicable as per Government of India norms.
